rationale.root_cause: They identified the unbounded copy at program.c:11 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) from argv into a 256-byte global buffer and explained it can exceed the buffer. They explicitly named it a global-buffer-overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed a bounded copy sized to the destination (snprintf with sizeof buf), ensuring NUL termination, and suggested rejecting/truncating inputs whose length >= sizeof buf before copying.
rationale.global_fix: They advised removing raw, unbounded string handling by using size-aware APIs, propagating buffer capacities, and avoiding fixed global buffers. This addresses the root cause beyond the single call site.
